verses.latin_vulgate: Væ qui prædaris ! nonne et ipse prædaberis ?  et qui spernis, nonne et ipse sperneris ?  Cum consummaveris deprædationem, deprædaberis ;  cum fatigatus desieris contemnere, contemneris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֣וֹי שׁוֹדֵ֗ד וְאַתָּה֙ לֹ֣א שָׁד֔וּד וּבוֹגֵ֖ד וְלֹא־בָ֣גְדוּ ב֑וֹ כַּהֲתִֽמְךָ֤ שׁוֹדֵד֙ תּוּשַּׁ֔ד כַּנְּלֹתְךָ֥ לִבְגֹּ֖ד יִבְגְּדוּ־בָֽךְ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Woe to thee that spoilest, and thou wast not spoiled; and dealest treacherously, and they dealt not treacherously with thee! when thou shalt cease to spoil, thou shalt be spoiled; and when thou shalt make an end to deal treacherously, they shall deal treacherously with thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Domine, miserere nostri,  te enim exspectavimus ;  esto brachium nostrum in mane,  et salus nostra in tempore tribulationis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְהֹוָ֥ה חׇנֵּ֖נוּ לְךָ֣ קִוִּ֑ינוּ הֱיֵ֤ה זְרֹעָם֙ לַבְּקָרִ֔ים אַף־יְשׁוּעָתֵ֖נוּ בְּעֵ֥ת צָרָֽה׃
verses.king_james: O Lord, be gracious unto us; we have waited for thee: be thou their arm every morning, our salvation also in the time of trouble.

verses.latin_vulgate: A voce angeli fugerunt populi,  et ab exaltatione tua dispersæ sunt gentes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִקּ֣וֹל הָמ֔וֹן נָדְד֖וּ עַמִּ֑ים מֵר֣וֹמְמֻתֶ֔ךָ נָפְצ֖וּ גּוֹיִֽם׃
verses.king_james: At the noise of the tumult the people fled; at the lifting up of thyself the nations were scattered.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et congregabuntur spolia vestra sicut colligitur bruchus,  velut cum fossæ plenæ fuerint de eo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֻסַּ֣ף שְׁלַלְכֶ֔ם אֹ֖סֶף הֶחָסִ֑יל כְּמַשַּׁ֥ק גֵּבִ֖ים שֹׁקֵ֥ק בּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: And your spoil shall be gathered like the gathering of the caterpiller: as the running to and fro of locusts shall he run upon them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Magnificatus est Dominus, quoniam habitavit in excelso ;  implevit Sion judicio et justitia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נִשְׂגָּ֣ב יְהֹוָ֔ה כִּ֥י שֹׁכֵ֖ן מָר֑וֹם מִלֵּ֣א צִיּ֔וֹן מִשְׁפָּ֖ט וּצְדָקָֽה׃
verses.king_james: The Lord is exalted; for he dwelleth on high: he hath filled Zion with judgment and righteousness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erit fides in temporibus tuis :  divitiæ salutis sapientia et scientia ;  timor Domini ipse est thesaurus ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָה֙ אֱמוּנַ֣ת עִתֶּ֔יךָ חֹ֥סֶן יְשׁוּעֹ֖ת חׇכְמַ֣ת וָדָ֑עַת יִרְאַ֥ת יְהֹוָ֖ה הִ֥יא אוֹצָרֽוֹ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: And wisdom and knowledge shall be the stability of thy times, and strength of salvation: the fear of the Lord is his treasure.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce videntes clamabunt foris ;  angeli pacis amare flebunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֚ן אֶרְאֶלָּ֔ם צָעֲק֖וּ חֻ֑צָה מַלְאֲכֵ֣י שָׁל֔וֹם מַ֖ר יִבְכָּיֽוּן׃
verses.king_james: Behold, their valiant ones shall cry without: the ambassadors of peace shall weep bitterly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dissipatæ sunt viæ,  cessavit transiens per semitam :  irritum factum est pactum, projecit civitates,  non reputavit homines.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נָשַׁ֣מּוּ מְסִלּ֔וֹת שָׁבַ֖ת עֹבֵ֣ר אֹ֑רַח הֵפֵ֤ר בְּרִית֙ מָאַ֣ס עָרִ֔ים לֹ֥א חָשַׁ֖ב אֱנֽוֹשׁ׃
verses.king_james: The highways lie waste, the wayfaring man ceaseth: he hath broken the covenant, he hath despised the cities, he regardeth no man.

verses.latin_vulgate: Luxit et elanguit terra ;  confusus est Libanus, et obsorduit :  et factus est Saron sicut desertum,  et concussa est Basan, et Carmelus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָבַ֤ל אֻמְלְלָה֙ אָ֔רֶץ הֶחְפִּ֥יר לְבָנ֖וֹן קָמַ֑ל הָיָ֤ה הַשָּׁרוֹן֙ כָּֽעֲרָבָ֔ה וְנֹעֵ֥ר בָּשָׁ֖ן וְכַרְמֶֽל׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: The earth mourneth and languisheth: Lebanon is ashamed and hewn down: Sharon is like a wilderness; and Bashan and Carmel shake off their fruits.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc consurgam, dicit Dominus ;  nunc exaltabor, nunc sublevabor.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עַתָּ֥ה אָק֖וּם יֹאמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֑ה עַתָּה֙ אֵֽרוֹמָ֔ם עַתָּ֖ה אֶנָּשֵֽׂא׃
verses.king_james: Now will I rise, saith the Lord; now will I be exalted; now will I lift up myself.

verses.latin_vulgate: Concipietis ardorem, parietis stipulam ;  spiritus vester ut ignis vorabit vos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תַּהֲר֥וּ חֲשַׁ֖שׁ תֵּ֣לְדוּ קַ֑שׁ רוּחֲכֶ֕ם אֵ֖שׁ תֹּאכַלְכֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: Ye shall conceive chaff, ye shall bring forth stubble: your breath, as fire, shall devour you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erunt populi quasi de incendio cinis ;  spinæ congregatæ igni comburentur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָי֥וּ עַמִּ֖ים מִשְׂרְפ֣וֹת שִׂ֑יד קוֹצִ֥ים כְּסוּחִ֖ים בָּאֵ֥שׁ יִצַּֽתּוּ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: And the people shall be as the burnings of lime: as thorns cut up shall they be burned in the fire.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audite, qui longe estis, quæ fecerim ;  et cognoscite, vicini, fortitudinem meam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁמְע֥וּ רְחוֹקִ֖ים אֲשֶׁ֣ר עָשִׂ֑יתִי וּדְע֥וּ קְרוֹבִ֖ים גְּבֻרָתִֽי׃
verses.king_james: Hear, ye that are far off, what I have done; and, ye that are near, acknowledge my might.

verses.latin_vulgate: Conterriti sunt in Sion peccatores ;  possedit tremor hypocritas.  Quis poterit habitare de vobis cum igne devorante ?  quis habitabit ex vobis cum ardoribus sempiternis ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פָּחֲד֤וּ בְצִיּוֹן֙ חַטָּאִ֔ים אָחֲזָ֥ה רְעָדָ֖ה חֲנֵפִ֑ים מִ֣י ׀ יָג֣וּר לָ֗נוּ אֵ֚שׁ אֽוֹכֵלָ֔ה מִֽי־יָג֥וּר לָ֖נוּ מוֹקְדֵ֥י עוֹלָֽם׃
verses.king_james: The sinners in Zion are afraid; fearfulness hath surprised the hypocrites. Who among us shall dwell with the devouring fire? who among us shall dwell with everlasting burnings?

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui ambulat in justitiis et loquitur veritatem,  qui projicit avaritiam ex calumnia,  et excutit manus suas ab omni munere,  qui obturat aures suas ne audiat sanguinem,  et claudit oculos suos ne videat malum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֹלֵ֣ךְ צְדָק֔וֹת וְדֹבֵ֖ר מֵישָׁרִ֑ים מֹאֵ֞ס בְּבֶ֣צַע מַעֲשַׁקּ֗וֹת נֹעֵ֤ר כַּפָּיו֙ מִתְּמֹ֣ךְ בַּשֹּׁ֔חַד אֹטֵ֤ם אׇזְנוֹ֙ מִשְּׁמֹ֣עַ דָּמִ֔ים וְעֹצֵ֥ם עֵינָ֖יו מֵרְא֥וֹת בְּרָֽע׃
verses.king_james: He that walketh righteously, and speaketh uprightly; he that despiseth the gain of oppressions, that shaketh his hands from holding of bribes, that stoppeth his ears from hearing of blood, and shutteth his eyes from seeing evil;

verses.latin_vulgate: Iste in excelsis habitabit ;  munimenta saxorum sublimitas ejus :  panis ei datus est, aquæ ejus fideles sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֚וּא מְרוֹמִ֣ים יִשְׁכֹּ֔ן מְצָד֥וֹת סְלָעִ֖ים מִשְׂגַּבּ֑וֹ לַחְמ֣וֹ נִתָּ֔ן מֵימָ֖יו נֶאֱמָנִֽים׃
verses.king_james: He shall dwell on high: his place of defence shall be the munitions of rocks: bread shall be given him; his waters shall be sure.

verses.latin_vulgate: Regem in decore suo videbunt oculi ejus,  cernent terram de longe.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֶ֥לֶךְ בְּיׇפְי֖וֹ תֶּחֱזֶ֣ינָה עֵינֶ֑יךָ תִּרְאֶ֖ינָה אֶ֥רֶץ מַרְחַקִּֽים׃
verses.king_james: Thine eyes shall see the king in his beauty: they shall behold the land that is very far off.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cor tuum meditabitur timorem :  ubi est litteratus ? ubi legis verba ponderans ?  ubi doctor parvulorum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לִבְּךָ֖ יֶהְגֶּ֣ה אֵימָ֑ה אַיֵּ֤ה סֹפֵר֙ אַיֵּ֣ה שֹׁקֵ֔ל אַיֵּ֖ה סֹפֵ֥ר אֶת־הַמִּגְדָּלִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Thine heart shall meditate terror. Where is the scribe? where is the receiver? where is he that counted the towers?

verses.latin_vulgate: Populum impudentem non videbis,  populum alti sermonis, ita ut non possis intelligere  disertitudinem linguæ ejus, in quo nulla est sapientia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶת־עַ֥ם נוֹעָ֖ז לֹ֣א תִרְאֶ֑ה עַ֣ם עִמְקֵ֤י שָׂפָה֙ מִשְּׁמ֔וֹעַ נִלְעַ֥ג לָשׁ֖וֹן אֵ֥ין בִּינָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Thou shalt not see a fierce people, a people of a deeper speech than thou canst perceive; of a stammering tongue, that thou canst not understand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respice, Sion, civitatem solemnitatis nostræ :  oculi tui videbunt Jerusalem,  habitationem opulentam,  tabernaculum quod nequaquam transferri poterit ;  nec auferentur clavi ejus in sempiternum,  et omnes funiculi ejus non rumpentur :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חֲזֵ֣ה צִיּ֔וֹן קִרְיַ֖ת מוֹעֲדֵ֑נוּ עֵינֶ֩יךָ֩ תִרְאֶ֨ינָה יְרוּשָׁלַ֜͏ִם נָוֶ֣ה שַׁאֲנָ֗ן אֹ֤הֶל בַּל־יִצְעָן֙ בַּל־יִסַּ֤ע יְתֵדֹתָיו֙ לָנֶ֔צַח וְכׇל־חֲבָלָ֖יו בַּל־יִנָּתֵֽקוּ׃
verses.king_james: Look upon Zion, the city of our solemnities: thine eyes shall see Jerusalem a quiet habitation, a tabernacle that shall not be taken down; not one of the stakes thereof shall ever be removed, neither shall any of the cords thereof be broken.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia solummodo ibi magnificus est Dominus noster :  locus fluviorum rivi latissimi et patentes :  non transibit per eum navis remigum,  neque trieris magna transgredietur eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י אִם־שָׁ֞ם אַדִּ֤יר יְהֹוָה֙ לָ֔נוּ מְקוֹם־נְהָרִ֥ים יְאֹרִ֖ים רַחֲבֵ֣י יָדָ֑יִם בַּל־תֵּ֤לֶךְ בּוֹ֙ אֳנִי־שַׁ֔יִט וְצִ֥י אַדִּ֖יר לֹ֥א יַעַבְרֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.king_james: But there the glorious Lord will be unto us a place of broad rivers and streams; wherein shall go no galley with oars, neither shall gallant ship pass thereby.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dominus enim judex noster,  Dominus legifer noster,  Dominus rex noster,  ipse salvabit nos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י יְהֹוָה֙ שֹׁפְטֵ֔נוּ יְהֹוָ֖ה מְחֹקְקֵ֑נוּ יְהֹוָ֥ה מַלְכֵּ֖נוּ ה֥וּא יוֹשִׁיעֵֽנוּ׃
verses.king_james: For the Lord is our judge, the Lord is our lawgiver, the Lord is our king; he will save us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Laxati sunt funiculi tui,  et non prævalebunt ;  sic erit malus tuus  ut dilatare signum non queas.  Tunc dividentur spolia prædarum multarum ;  claudi diripient rapinam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נִטְּשׁ֖וּ חֲבָלָ֑יִךְ בַּל־יְחַזְּק֤וּ כֵן־תׇּרְנָם֙ בַּל־פָּ֣רְשׂוּ נֵ֔ס אָ֣ז חֻלַּ֤ק עַד־שָׁלָל֙ מַרְבֶּ֔ה פִּסְחִ֖ים בָּ֥זְזוּ בַֽז׃
verses.king_james: Thy tacklings are loosed; they could not well strengthen their mast, they could not spread the sail: then is the prey of a great spoil divided; the lame take the prey.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nec dicet vicinus : Elangui ;  populus qui habitat in ea, auferetur ab eo iniquitas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבַל־יֹאמַ֥ר שָׁכֵ֖ן חָלִ֑יתִי הָעָ֛ם הַיֹּשֵׁ֥ב בָּ֖הּ נְשֻׂ֥א עָוֺֽן׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: And the inhabitant shall not say, I am sick: the people that dwell therein shall be forgiven their iniquity.
